input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on macOS (iOS + Android):

---

## 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers/linkers |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI |
| **MAUI workload** | `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI templates & build targets |
| **Visual Studio Code** (optional) | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) | Editor with C# Dev Kit |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by the MAUI workload via Android SDK | Android builds |

---

## 2. Step-by-Step

1. **Xcode** — Install, then open once to accept the license. Run:
   ```
   sudo xcodebuild -license accept
   sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
   ```

2. **.NET SDK** — Download the macOS (Arm64 or x64) installer from [dot.net](https://dot.net/download). Pick the latest supported version (currently .NET 8 LTS or .NET 9).

3. **MAUI workload**:
   ```
   dotnet workload install maui
   ```
   This pulls in Android SDK, Android emulator, build tools, and iOS tooling.

4. **Android SDK/Emulator** — The workload installs a private Android SDK. To manage it manually or add system images:
   ```
   dotnet android sdk list
   dotnet android sdk install "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
   ```
   Or use Android Studio's SDK Manager ([developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)).

---

## 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See installed .NET SDKs and runtimes
dotnet --info

# See installed workloads and available updates
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload update

# Check Xcode version (must meet MAUI's minimum)
xcodebuild -version

# Check MAUI's required Xcode version
dotnet workload search maui   # or see release notes
```

Consult the official support matrix:  
[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)

---

## 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android Emulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

If all four commands succeed, your environment is fully functional.

---

## Key Notes

- **No Visual Studio for Mac needed** — it's been retired. Use VS Code + C# Dev Kit or JetBrains Rider.
- **Physical iOS device** requires an Apple Developer account and provisioning profile.
- **Apple Silicon** — use the Arm64 .NET installer for best performance.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current with SDK/platform patches.
